title: Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore)
summary: The Centre for International Law (CIL) at the National University of Singapore is inviting applications for Postdoctoral Fellowship positions commencing in September 2026. This is a research-focused opportunity designed for early-career scholars aiming to pursue academic careers in international law. About the Centre for International Law (CIL) The Centre for International Law is a university-level research centre dedicated to producing cutting-edge scholarship in international law. It plays a leading role in capacity-building across the Asia-Pacific region through: Academic workshops and conferences Professional training sessions Research-driven programmes engaging academics, government officials, and private sector professionals CIL is also strongly oriented toward supporting academic development and policy-relevant research in international law. Fellowship Overview The Postdoctoral Fellowship is a one-year appointment, with the possibility of renewal for a second year based on satisfactory performance during the first year. Start date: September 2026 Duration: 1 year (renewable for a second year) Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore Employee category: Research Staff Posted on: 19 January 2026 The programme is directed by Dr. Nilufer Oral (Director of CIL and member of the International Law Commission), together with Professor Joseph Weiler, visiting professor at NUS. Research Areas Applications are open to candidates working in all areas of international law. However, special consideration will be given to research projects focused on: The Asia-Pacific region Climate change law ASEAN law and policy Fellows are encouraged to pursue research that is both academically rigorous and policy relevant. Programme Objectives and Academic Environment The fellowship is specifically designed to support scholars pursuing academic careers. Fellows will benefit from: Individual academic mentoring Workshops on research design and academic writing Training in publication strategies and teaching methods Guidance on supervision and methodological development Fellows will also actively participate in the academic life of CIL, including presenting research, contributing to discussions, and engaging with visiting scholars. Qualifications and Selection Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: A PhD in international law obtained within the last two years OR Doctoral candidates who will complete their dissertation by 31 July 2026 Demonstrated research excellence and originality in the doctoral dissertation Strong academic potential and a well-developed postdoctoral research proposal Required Submission Materials: PhD dissertation (PDF) Postdoctoral research proposal One or two samples of published work Letters of Recommendation: Three confidential reference letters are required Ideally, one should be from the PhD supervisor or examiner Referees must send letters directly to: huong.tam@nus.edu.sg References must assess: Dissertation quality Proposed research project Academic potential Application Procedure Applications must be submitted online via the NUS career portal. Deadlines: Preferred deadline: 30 April 2026 Final deadline: 15 May 2026 Required Documents: Personal statement (500–600 words) outlining motivation, career goals, and relevant background Full curriculum vitae (including education, employment history, publications, awards, and honours) PhD dissertation and doctoral certification Research proposal (maximum 3000 words), including: Research questions Methodology Expected contribution to the field Anticipated outputs (e.g., journal articles, book manuscript) Executive summary (maximum 600 words) One or two writing samples Three academic references (submitted separately) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Programme Expectations Selected Fellows are expected to: Carry out the proposed research project during the fellowship period Publish or secure acceptance for at least one or two peer-reviewed articles (or a book proposal with a reputable publisher) Participate actively in workshops and academic discussions Present research at conferences, including at NUS and international venues Be in residence at NUS for the duration of the appointment (with limited approved travel for research and fieldwork) Standard NUS leave policies apply. Additional Information Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore Organization: Centre for International Law, NUS Job Category: Research Staff Reference ID: 31488 Application status: Open until 15 May 2026 Notification: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare all required documents and submit their application through the official NUS career portal before the deadline. Early submission is strongly encouraged due to preference for applications received by 30 April 2026. APPLY HERE Discover more similar opportunities Here. The post Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: $10,000 UpGreyed Her Grant for Women-Led Startups in 2026
summary: Women entrepreneurs continue to face a significant funding gap—estimated at $1.9 trillion in 2026 , up from $1.7 trillion the previous year. To help bridge this gap, Grey has launched its global UpGreyed Her initiative, offering $10,000 in equity-free grants to support women-led businesses across emerging markets and beyond. About the UpGreyed Her Initiative UpGreyed Her is a global program designed to scale women-led businesses by providing financial support and access to infrastructure that fuels growth. The initiative focuses on empowering women entrepreneurs who are leveraging technology to solve real-world problems. Grey emphasizes that investing in women founders is not just about closing a gap—it’s about unlocking untapped potential and driving economic growth across regions. By supporting startups in cities like Lagos, São Paulo, and Jakarta, the initiative aims to multiply impact across entire economies. Grant Details A total of $10,000 in equity-free funding will be awarded to three outstanding women founders: 1st Place: $5,000 1st Runner-Up: $3,000 2nd Runner-Up: $2,000 This funding comes with no equity requirements , allowing founders to grow their businesses freely. Why You Should Apply Gain equity-free funding to scale your business Join a global network of women founders Increase your visibility and credibility Access support from a trusted financial platform Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the UpGreyed Her grant, applicants must meet the following requirements: The business must be founded by a woman or have a female co-founder with at least 50% ownership The company must have been operational for at least 3 years The founder must be between 23 and 40 years old Applicants must be citizens or residents of eligible countries across: Africa The Middle East Southeast Asia Europe Latin America The UK The US The business must use technology to solve real-world problems and be ready to scale Read terms and conditions here. Application Timeline Applications Open: March 18, 2026 Deadline: April 20, 2026 (by 9:00 AM Pacific Time) Early applications are strongly encouraged to avoid last-minute issues. Transparent Evaluation Process Applications will be reviewed by a panel of accomplished female judges with expertise in business, technology, and management. The evaluation process is designed to be fair, transparent, and focused on identifying high-impact ventures with strong growth potential. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official platform before the deadline. Be sure to review the full eligibility list and terms and conditions prior to applying. APPLY HERE Pro Tip Focus your application on demonstrating measurable impact and scalability . Clearly show how your technology solves a real problem and how the grant will accelerate your next stage of growth. Discover similar Grant opportunities Here. title: SIEF Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for EdTech in Foundational Learning
summary: The Strategic Impact Evaluation Fund (SIEF) has officially launched its eighth Call for Proposals , focusing on the role of education technology (EdTech) in improving children’s foundational learning. This initiative aims to support innovative, evidence-based solutions that can strengthen early learning outcomes worldwide. As digital tools continue to reshape education, this call emphasizes the need for rigorous, policy-relevant research to determine what truly works in EdTech. About the Program Hosted by the World Bank , SIEF is dedicated to funding impact evaluations that generate reliable evidence for policymakers. This latest call centers on “nimble evaluations” —rapid and cost-effective studies designed to test how technology can accelerate foundational learning and skills development among children. Why This Matters EdTech has gained global attention as a scalable and cost-effective solution for improving education. While some interventions have shown strong results, the broader evidence remains mixed, with many programs yielding only modest or no impact. At the same time, emerging technologies—such as affordable digital devices and artificial intelligence —are expanding possibilities in education. This makes it increasingly important to evaluate which innovations truly deliver results , especially in foundational literacy and numeracy. Key Focus Areas SIEF Call 8 prioritizes: Rapid, low-cost evaluations of EdTech interventions Evidence generation to inform education policy Solutions that improve foundational learning outcomes Scalable and impactful digital education tools Benefits of Applying Access to research funding for EdTech evaluations Opportunity to influence global education policy Collaboration with the World Bank and leading researchers Contribution to improving children’s foundational learning outcomes worldwide Focus your proposal on clear, measurable outcomes and ensure your evaluation design is cost-effective and scalable . Strong proposals often combine innovation with practical policy relevance . Eligibility Application Process There are two main application pathways: 1. Quick Assessment Form For external researchers not currently collaborating with a World Bank Task Team Leader (TTL) Deadline: April 22, 2026 2. Main Application Form To be submitted by a World Bank Task Team Leader (TTL) Deadlin e: May 19, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the full call documents and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before applying. How to Apply Interested applicants can access the following: Call for proposals Quick Assessment Form Main Application Form Submission links for both application stages For additional inquiries, applicants can contact the SIEF team via email at siefimpact@worldbank.org with the subject line: “SIEF Call 8 query.” Conclusion The SIEF Call for Proposals 8 presents a unique opportunity for researchers and policymakers to advance evidence-based EdTech solutions . With a strong emphasis on rapid evaluation and real-world impact, this initiative aims to shape the future of foundational learning globally. Discover similar opportunities Here. The post SIEF Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for EdTech in Foundational Learning appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026: Fully Funded International Research Opportunity in Germany for Global Scholars
summary: The Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026 is a highly competitive international research funding opportunity designed to foster global academic collaboration and interdisciplinary innovation. Hosted by the Bayreuth Humboldt Centre at the University of Bayreuth in Germany, this program provides financial support for short-term research visits aimed at strengthening partnerships between international scholars and German academics. This grant is ideal for researchers seeking to expand their academic networks, engage in collaborative projects, and contribute to cutting-edge research in a globally recognized academic environment. Overview of the Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026 The Short Term Grants program supports international researchers to undertake short research stays (1–3 weeks) at the University of Bayreuth. The initiative is part of the Centre’s broader mission to promote excellence in research and international academic exchange. The program emphasizes: High-quality collaborative research Interdisciplinary engagement Long-term academic partnerships Global knowledge exchange Key Features of the Programme 1. Short Research Duration Research stay duration: 1 to 3 weeks Focus on intensive collaboration and academic exchange 2. Financial Support Successful applicants receive funding of up to €3,500, which covers: International travel expenses (home country to Germany and return) Accommodation in Bayreuth Visa fees (if applicable) Daily allowance for living expenses 3. Open to All Academic Disciplines The grant is not restricted by field Encourages interdisciplinary and cross-sector collaboration 4. Strong Focus on International Collaboration Promotes partnerships between: International researchers Faculty members at the University of Bayreuth Supports both early-career and established researchers What the Grant Supports The program is structured to facilitate: Joint research projects Academic networking and collaboration Exchange of knowledge and expertise Development of long-term research partnerships Cross-cultural academic engagement Role of the Host Institution A key requirement is securing a host researcher at the University of Bayreuth prior to applying. Host Responsibilities: Provide access to office or laboratory space Facilitate integration into the academic environment Support the research collaboration process Important: The Centre does not assign hosts Applicants must independently identify and contact a suitable host Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply (Category of People) International researchers and scholars Both early-career and senior researchers (excluding PhD students) Academic Qualifications Applicants must: Have completed a PhD (doctoral degree) Demonstrate strong academic and research background Residency Requirements Must have lived outside Germany for at least 12 months within the last 18 months prior to application Additional Conditions Researchers currently based in Germany are not eligible PhD students cannot apply Eligible Countries and Regions Open to applicants from all countries worldwide No geographic restrictions (global eligibility) Particularly suitable for researchers from: Africa Asia Europe (outside Germany) Americas Oceania The program is explicitly designed to strengthen international academic collaboration globally. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Academic excellence of the applicant Quality and feasibility of the proposed research project Strength of collaboration with the host institution Potential for long-term academic partnerships and networking Duration and Timeline Grant duration: 1–3 weeks Earliest start: Approximately 1 month after acceptance Latest start: Up to 9 months after acceptance Why You Should Apply The Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026 offers: Access to world-class research facilities in Germany Opportunities to collaborate with leading scholars Financial support covering key research expenses Enhanced academic visibility and global networking A pathway to future collaborations and funding opportunities Application Process Applicants must: Identify and secure a host at the University of Bayreuth Prepare a strong research proposal Complete the online application form Submit all required documents before the deadline Strict adherence to application guidelines is required, and incomplete submissions are automatically disqualified. Apply here Application Deadline 30th April 2026 The Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026 stands out as a prestigious and accessible research grant for international scholars seeking short-term academic engagement in Germany. With generous funding, global eligibility, and a focus on collaboration, this opportunity is ideal for researchers aiming to expand their academic impact and international networks. If you are a qualified researcher with a strong project idea and a potential academic partner in Germany, this grant provides a strategic platform to advance your research career on a global scale. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Bayreuth Humboldt Centre Short Term Grants 2026: Fully Funded International Research Opportunity in Germany for Global Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: NYCC Climate Champions Fellowship 2026 for Young Climate Leaders
summary: Are you passionate about climate action and ready to drive real change in your community? The NYCC Climate Champions Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications! This flagship youth program empowers young leaders across India to take meaningful grassroots climate action through leadership, advocacy, and community engagement. What is the NYCC Climate Champions Fellowship? The NYCC Climate Champions Fellowship is a transformative initiative designed to support and equip young changemakers with the tools, mentorship, and resources needed to address climate challenges at the grassroots level. Led by Bring Back Green Foundation and supported by UNICEF YuWaah , the program emphasizes youth-led action and collaboration with government and local stakeholders for sustainable impact. Over the past two years, the fellowship has: Trained 62 Climate Champions Covered 33 States Union Territories in India Built strong local partnerships Enabled fellows to represent their work at national and international platforms What makes this fellowship unique is its strong focus on localized solutions and youth-government partnerships , ensuring impactful and scalable climate initiatives. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will gain access to: Grants for Project Implementation Funding support to execute your climate project Training Mentorship Learn from climate experts and experienced mentors National International Exposure Opportunities to showcase your work on bigger platforms 6-Month Structured Fellowship A guided timeline to develop and implement your ideas Networking Opportunities Collaborate with like-minded youth and organizations Focus Areas Fellows will work on projects across key climate action areas, including: Research Innovation Network Mobilization Awareness Capacity Building Eligibility Criteria To apply, you must: Be 18 to 29 years old Have a strong interest in climate action and community engagement Be committed to implementing a local climate project Selection Process The fellowship follows a three-stage selection process: Stage 1: Application Screening Top applications are shortlisted. AI-generated or incomplete submissions are automatically disqualified. Stage 2: Project Proposal Submission Shortlisted candidates submit a detailed proposal outlining their climate project. Stage 3: Virtual Interview Finalists are interviewed and selected based on: Commitment Project feasibility Potential impact How to Apply Applications are submitted via an online Google Form You can apply in English, Hindi, or your preferred regional language For guidance, download and review the Fellowship Handbook Application Forms: English Version Hindi Version Deadline Last Date to Apply: 3rd May 2026 Pro Tip Before applying, take time to develop a clear, realistic, and community-focused project idea . Strong proposals that demonstrate local impact and sustainability have a higher chance of being selected. If you’re a young changemaker eager to tackle climate challenges and create lasting impact, this is your opportunity to step up and lead. Join a growing network of climate champions and be part of a movement shaping a sustainable future. Apply now and become the next Climate Champion! Discover similar fellowship opportunities Here. title: Metropolis Mentoring Program 2026: Migration Mentorship Opportunity for Emerging Leaders
summary: The Metropolis Mentoring Program 2026 is a professional development opportunity for emerging leaders and experienced practitioners seeking mentorship and growth in migration, settlement, and multicultural affairs. Designed as a structured pilot initiative by the Metropolis International Migration Network in partnership with SSI, the program aims to strengthen leadership capacity across the global migration sector through guided knowledge-sharing and meaningful professional connections. About the Program The Metropolis Mentoring Program Pilot is a newly launched initiative that connects early-career professionals (mentees) with experienced experts (mentors) working across migration, settlement services, public policy, research, civil society, and related fields. Running from February to December 2026, the program is designed to foster structured, goal-oriented mentoring relationships that support leadership development and sector advancement. Each mentoring pair will be carefully matched based on professional experience, thematic interests, and development goals. The program focuses on building practical skills, enhancing leadership capacity, and strengthening networks across migration and multicultural sectors globally. The pilot cohort will include six mentoring pairs, with structured engagement supported by session templates, conversation frameworks, and curated resources to ensure consistency and impact throughout the program. Why This Opportunity Matters Migration systems worldwide are increasingly complex, requiring leaders who can respond to displacement, diversity, and integration challenges with evidence-based and inclusive approaches. However, many emerging professionals lack access to experienced mentors who can guide them through sector-specific challenges and career pathways. This program addresses that gap by creating structured mentorship relationships that support reflective practice, knowledge transfer, and leadership development. By connecting professionals across government, academia, community organisations, and international institutions, the initiative strengthens collaboration and improves the overall capacity of the migration and settlement sector. It also contributes to building more equitable leadership pathways by supporting diverse voices, including those with lived experience of migration and displacement, Indigenous perspectives, and diaspora leadership. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the Metropolis Mentoring Program 2026 will benefit from structured, meaningful professional development designed to support long-term career growth and sector impact. Mentees will gain personalised guidance tailored to their career goals, helping them navigate professional challenges, strengthen leadership skills, and build confidence in their chosen field. They will also develop clearer career direction through regular reflective conversations and practical advice from experienced mentors. Mentors will have the opportunity to contribute to leadership development in the migration sector while sharing expertise, expanding their professional networks, and engaging in reflective practice that strengthens their own leadership approach. Both mentors and mentees will benefit from structured sessions supported by conversation frameworks and tools designed to ensure productive engagement. The program also encourages networking within the wider Metropolis community, offering additional opportunities for professional exchange and collaboration. Who Can Apply The program is open to professionals working in migration, settlement, multicultural affairs, and related sectors. This includes individuals working in academic and research institutions, government agencies, non-governmental organisations, civil society, private sector organisations, and international bodies. Mentees are typically early-career professionals seeking guidance to advance their work and leadership development. Mentors are experienced practitioners committed to supporting the growth of emerging leaders through structured knowledge-sharing and professional mentorship. Eligible Areas Participants may work across a range of thematic areas, including women in leadership and migration policy, lived experience of displacement or migration, Indigenous and decolonial perspectives, and diaspora and community leadership. These themes ensure the program reflects diverse perspectives and addresses key challenges in global migration systems. Application Process and Deadline Applications for the Metropolis Mentoring Program Pilot are open through an Expression of Interest process. Applicants are required to submit their interest indicating whether they wish to participate as a mentor or mentee. The program timeline begins with applications closing on 30 April 2026, followed by a program launch in February 2026 and completion in December 2026. Selected participants will be matched based on professional alignment, development needs, availability, and thematic focus areas. Mentoring pairs are expected to engage in approximately 3–6 structured sessions throughout the year, conducted either online or in person where possible. These sessions will include an introductory meeting, core developmental discussions, and a final reflection session to consolidate learning and outcomes. Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the limited number of available mentoring pairs in this pilot phase. Learn more and apply on the official SSI website . title: U.S. Climate Alliance Federal and External Affairs Internship 2026: Fully Remote Paid Climate Policy Internship Opportunity for Students and Graduates
summary: The U.S. Climate Alliance Federal and External Affairs Internship 2026 presents a highly competitive and impactful opportunity for students and recent graduates seeking hands-on experience in climate policy, environmental governance, and public sector collaboration. This paid, part-time, and fully remote internship offers participants a rare chance to work at the intersection of state and federal climate policy in the United States while contributing to meaningful climate action initiatives. Overview of the U.S. Climate Alliance The U.S. Climate Alliance is a bipartisan coalition of governors committed to advancing state-led, high-impact climate solutions aimed at achieving a net-zero future for the United States. Through its Secretariat, the Alliance provides technical expertise, policy analysis, coordination support, and capacity-building resources to member states and territories. The organization plays a central role in bridging state-level initiatives with federal policy frameworks while collaborating with national partners to accelerate climate resilience, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and support a sustainable economic transition. Internship Details Position Title: Federal and External Affairs Intern Organization: U.S. Climate Alliance Secretariat Location: Remote (U.S.-based organization) Duration: May 26, 2026 – August 18, 2026 Work Schedule: 20–29 hours per week (fixed schedule) Compensation: $17.95 – $20.00 per hour This internship is structured to provide practical, project-based learning experiences in climate policy research, tracking, and coordination. Interns will work collaboratively with Alliance staff, consultants, external partners, and government stakeholders. Key Responsibilities Interns will engage in a wide range of activities that directly support climate policy coordination and implementation, including: 1. Climate Policy Research and Tracking Monitor and track state-level climate policy developments Maintain and update the Alliance’s internal policy database Analyze policy implementation progress across member states 2. Federal Policy Monitoring Track federal climate initiatives, funding opportunities, and policy announcements Identify relevant public comment periods and regulatory developments Provide summaries and insights to inform internal strategy 3. Program and Event Support Assist in preparing materials for meetings, workshops, and peer-learning sessions Support coordination of multi-state collaboration efforts Contribute to internal and external engagement initiatives 4. Policy Communication and Content Development Draft newsletters, policy briefs, and internal updates Translate complex policy information into accessible content Support communication efforts with member states and partners Learning Outcomes and Career Benefits By the end of the internship, participants will gain: Practical experience in state and federal climate policy analysis Enhanced research, writing, and analytical skills Exposure to coalition-based policymaking and governance Insight into how complex policy frameworks are operationalized Professional experience working in a high-impact policy environment This internship is ideal for individuals seeking careers in environmental policy, public administration, climate advocacy, or international development. Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Undergraduate students currently enrolled in a degree program Graduate students pursuing advanced studies Recent college graduates with relevant interest or experience Academic and Professional Background Applicants should demonstrate: Academic or extracurricular involvement in climate, environmental studies, or public policy Interest in state-level and federal climate policy (preferred but not mandatory) Strong analytical, research, and communication abilities Apply here Required Skills and Competencies Successful candidates will possess: Strong research and analytical skills Ability to synthesize complex information efficiently Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong organizational and time management abilities Interest in collaborative, coalition-based work environments Professionalism, reliability, and adaptability Fluency in English is required. Eligible Countries and Regions Although the internship is remote, it is designed within a U.S. policy framework. Therefore: Applicants from the United States are strongly encouraged to apply International applicants may apply if they have strong knowledge or academic background in U.S. climate policy or related fields Candidates from all continents with relevant expertise and the ability to work remotely within the program’s time structure may be considered. Accessibility and Equal Opportunity The organization is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. Reasonable accommodations are provided in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA). The United Nations Foundation, under which the Secretariat operates, is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. Why You Should Apply This internship offers a unique platform to: Engage directly with real-world climate policy development Build a strong foundation for careers in environmental governance and policy analysis Work alongside experienced professionals and policymakers Contribute to impactful initiatives addressing climate change and sustainability Deadline 30th April 2026 The U.S. Climate Alliance Federal and External Affairs Internship 2026 stands out as a premier opportunity for aspiring climate leaders. With its strong focus on policy, collaboration, and real-world impact, it equips participants with the tools and experience needed to thrive in the evolving landscape of climate governance. If you are passionate about climate action, policy research, and collaborative problem-solving, this internship provides a powerful stepping stone toward a meaningful and impactful career. title: Elevate Prize 2027 Nominations Open: $300,000 Funding and Global Support for Social Impact Leaders
summary: The Elevate Prize 2027 is now open for nominations, offering a global opportunity for social impact leaders who are driving transformative change within their communities and scaling solutions to address pressing global challenges. The prize provides both significant funding and long-term strategic support to help organizations expand their visibility, strengthen operations, and deepen impact. This opportunity is designed for visionary leaders and organizations committed to systemic and cultural change. It goes beyond a traditional award by combining unrestricted funding with a two-year tailored development program and access to a global network of changemakers. About the Program The Elevate Prize Foundation awards the Elevate Prize annually to exceptional social impact leaders whose work demonstrates bold vision, measurable impact, and the potential to scale globally. Nominations are open to individuals leading nonprofit organizations that are actively addressing social, environmental, or systemic challenges. The program selects leaders who are not only solving critical problems but also reshaping narratives and influencing broader systems of change. Winners are selected through a multi-stage evaluation process that includes nomination review, eligibility screening, application review, finalist interviews, and final selection. The program is structured around a two-year engagement period where winners receive tailored support across organizational development, visibility strategy, and leadership development. Even after the program concludes, winners remain part of a global alumni network that continues to provide opportunities for collaboration, learning, and engagement. Why This Opportunity Matters Social impact leaders often face significant challenges in scaling their work, including limited funding flexibility, lack of visibility, and constrained organizational capacity. The Elevate Prize addresses these barriers by providing unrestricted funding and long-term strategic support that enables leaders to focus on what matters most: impact. The program recognizes that transformative change requires both financial resources and sustained investment in leadership capacity. By combining funding with mentorship, visibility support, and community-building, the Elevate Prize helps organizations move from local impact to global influence. This opportunity is particularly valuable for leaders working on systemic issues such as education, health, climate, equity, and human rights, where long-term support and strategic partnerships are essential for meaningful change. What Participants Will Gain Selected Elevate Prize winners receive a comprehensive support package designed to accelerate both personal leadership and organizational growth. This includes $300,000 in unrestricted funding, with $250,000 allocated to the organization and $50,000 provided directly to the leader. Beyond funding, winners benefit from a tailored two-year support program focused on visibility, organizational development, and whole-leader well-being. This includes strategic guidance in areas such as branding, communications, digital presence, storytelling, partnerships, and public engagement. Participants also gain access to a global community of impact leaders, investors, and partners, along with exclusive convenings and an annual winners’ retreat. This network provides ongoing opportunities for collaboration, learning, and scaling impact beyond the program period. Who Can Apply The Elevate Prize is open to individuals who are at least 18 years old and serve in leadership roles such as Founder, CEO, Executive Director, or equivalent positions. Eligible applicants must represent a legally registered nonprofit organization in their country (or international equivalent of a U.S. 501(c)(3)). Organizations should have the capacity to receive philanthropic funding and actively implement programs with measurable impact. The program typically considers organizations with annual budgets between $2 million and $10 million, although organizations outside this range may still be considered based on readiness and impact potential. Applicants should demonstrate strong leadership, organizational momentum, and a clear vision for scaling their work. Eligible Areas This opportunity is open globally to nonprofit organizations working across sectors including but not limited to education, health, climate action, human rights, social justice, economic empowerment, and community development. Application Process and Deadline Nominations for the Elevate Prize are open to the public, and anyone can nominate a leader during the application window. Once nominated, selected candidates are invited to submit a full application. The selection process includes multiple stages: nominations, eligibility review, application submission, multi-round evaluation, finalist interviews, and final selection. Nominations are open until 8 May 2026 . Learn more and nominate someone you know on the official Elevate Prize website . title: AI for Reproductive Health in Sub-Saharan Africa Innovation Challenge 2026
summary: The AI for Reproductive Health Innovation Challenge 2026 offers an opportunity for multidisciplinary teams across Sub-Saharan Africa to develop AI-powered solutions addressing critical maternal and reproductive health challenges while gaining technical experience, mentorship, and funding support. Across Sub-Saharan Africa, reproductive health outcomes continue to be shaped by persistent systemic and behavioral challenges. Issues such as delayed care-seeking, limited access to accurate information, high contraceptive discontinuation, and gaps in post-pregnancy loss care highlight the urgent need for innovative solutions. This innovation challenge is designed for young professionals, researchers, and technologists who want to apply their skills to real-world health problems and contribute to improving health systems through technology. About the Program The AI for Reproductive Health Innovation Challenge is a regional initiative organized by the Infectious Diseases Institute (IDI) and the African Population and Health Research Center (APHRC), with support from international partners including AI4D, IDRC, and FCDO. The program brings together multidisciplinary teams to co-design, prototype, and test AI-enabled solutions using real-world datasets. Over the course of a four-week virtual innovation sprint, participants will engage in structured activities including a needs assessment, expert-led capacity-building sessions, regular mentorship check-ins, and final project presentations. Participants are required to select one of two thematic tracks. The first track focuses on improving access to comprehensive early pregnancy loss care, addressing gaps in service delivery, referral systems, and timely treatment. The second track centers on empowering informed contraceptive decision-making by developing intelligent tools that help individuals choose suitable methods based on their health profiles and personal circumstances. Why This Opportunity Matters Reproductive health challenges in Sub-Saharan Africa remain a significant public health concern, with preventable maternal deaths and limited access to essential services still prevalent in many regions. At the same time, advancements in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data science offer new possibilities to address these issues at scale. This innovation challenge creates a platform where technical expertise and health knowledge intersect. By bringing together professionals from diverse backgrounds, the program encourages the development of context-specific, culturally relevant, and scalable solutions. It also supports capacity building among African innovators, helping to strengthen local expertise and reduce reliance on external solutions. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the innovation challenge will benefit from a combination of practical experience, technical development, and professional exposure. Teams will gain hands-on experience working with real-world datasets and applying AI tools to solve complex health problems. The program provides access to mentorship from experts in data science, healthcare, and digital innovation, helping participants refine their ideas and improve the quality of their solutions. Capacity-building webinars and technical sessions will strengthen participants’ analytical and problem-solving skills, particularly in applying AI to health contexts. In addition to skill development, participants will have the opportunity to build meaningful collaborations across disciplines, expanding their professional networks. Winning teams will receive financial support and international exposure, including the opportunity to present their work at a major regional conference. Who Can Apply The innovation challenge is open to multidisciplinary teams composed of individuals with backgrounds in both technical and health-related fields. Teams must include members with expertise in areas such as data science, software engineering, artificial intelligence, medicine, nursing, or public health. Each team must consist of three to six members and include at least two female participants. Applicants must be residents or citizens of Sub-Saharan Africa and demonstrate a strong interest in using AI to address reproductive health challenges. Participants are expected to commit fully to the program, including attending all webinars, engaging in collaborative work, and participating in the final presentations. Eligible Areas This opportunity is open to applicants from all countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. Applications from Lusophone and Francophone countries are strongly encouraged to ensure regional diversity and inclusion. Application Process and Deadline Interested teams are required to submit an application that includes a 300-word statement of interest. This statement should outline the team’s motivation for applying, relevant experience in artificial intelligence and health, and how their proposed solution aligns with the challenge themes. Applications must be submitted by 3 May 2026 . Shortlisted teams will be invited to participate in the innovation sprint, which will take place from 11 May to 6 June 2026. Final presentations will be held on 11 June, with winners announced on 15 June 2026. Winning teams from each track will receive a cash prize of USD 1,350 and an all-expenses-paid opportunity to attend and present at Data Science Africa 2026, scheduled for 23–24 July at Makerere University in Kampala, Uganda. Learn more in the official call and apply in the Google Form. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post AI for Reproductive Health in Sub-Saharan Africa Innovation Challenge 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Eisner Prize Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded $50,000 Opportunity for Intergenerational Innovation Leaders in the United States
summary: The Eisner Foundation has officially opened applications for the highly competitive Eisner Prize Fellowship 2026–2027, a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed to support visionary leaders advancing intergenerational innovation. This fellowship stands out as one of the most impactful programs for individuals working at the intersection of youth and aging populations, providing both financial support and a powerful platform to scale transformative ideas. The fellowship is part of the broader Eisner Prize initiative, which has historically recognized excellence in bridging generational divides. In its fellowship format, the program goes further by investing directly in individuals with bold, actionable ideas that can reshape communities and create lasting societal impact. Fellowship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive support package valued at $50,000, structured as follows: $10,000 personal stipend (flexible use) $40,000 project funding to implement the proposed initiative Access to a network of experts and changemakers Participation in a fellowship convening in Los Angeles Ongoing virtual cohort sessions and mentorship Increased visibility and association with the prestigious Eisner Prize brand This combination of funding and professional exposure makes the fellowship particularly valuable for leaders seeking to scale innovative ideas. Who Can Apply (Eligible Categories of People) The Eisner Prize Fellowship targets individual leaders, not organizations. The ideal applicants include: Social entrepreneurs Researchers and academics Nonprofit professionals Innovators in technology, education, healthcare, or community development Students or early-career professionals with strong project ideas Importantly, the fellowship emphasizes diversity and inclusion, strongly encouraging applications from: Young leaders People of color Individuals working with historically marginalized communities Eligible Countries / Regions Primary Eligibility: United States only Applicants must be based in the United States They may be affiliated with a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization or apply with a fiscal sponsor This means that, at present, the opportunity is geographically limited and not open to international applicants outside the U.S. Qualification Requirements To qualify, applicants must: Propose a new and innovative project The project must focus on intergenerational connection (e.g., youth and older adults working together). It should introduce a new idea or expansion, not simply maintain an existing program. Demonstrate feasibility The project should be achievable within one year. It must be executable with the $40,000 project budget. Show leadership capacity Applicants must prove they have the skills, experience, or support network to successfully implement the project. Work independently or with organizational support While applicants may be affiliated with organizations, the project must be personally led by the fellow. Types of Projects Supported The fellowship welcomes a wide range of innovative ideas, including: Community-based intergenerational programs Research initiatives on aging and youth collaboration Technology platforms connecting generations Educational or mentorship programs Healthcare or social care innovations The key requirement is that the project must intentionally connect different generations in meaningful and measurable ways. Fellowship Structure and Experience Selected fellows will engage in a structured yet flexible program that includes: Kickoff Convening (Los Angeles): Fellows meet in person to launch their projects Monthly Virtual Sessions: Knowledge sharing, expert talks, and peer learning One-on-One Check-ins: Personalized support from the foundation Thought Leadership Opportunities: Fellows contribute blogs and insights This structure ensures that participants not only receive funding but also develop as leaders and innovators. Why You Should Apply The Eisner Prize Fellowship is more than a funding opportunity—it is a career-defining platform. Fellows gain: Credibility and recognition in the social impact space Access to influential networks The ability to test and scale innovative ideas Long-term association with a globally respected philanthropic institution For individuals passionate about solving societal challenges through intergenerational collaboration, this fellowship provides both resources and influence to make meaningful change. Application Process Applicants are required to submit an online application that may include: Project proposal Personal and professional background Optional supplementary materials (e.g., portfolios, recommendations, links to previous work) Apply here Deadline 10th May 2026 The Eisner Prize Fellowship 2026–2027 is a rare and powerful opportunity for individuals working to bridge generational divides and build inclusive communities. With substantial funding, leadership support, and national recognition, the program empowers fellows to transform ideas into impactful realities. If you are a changemaker with a bold vision for intergenerational innovation, this fellowship could be the catalyst that elevates your work to the next level. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Eisner Prize Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded $50,000 Opportunity for Intergenerational Innovation Leaders in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
